Title: HCCF’s Vision for a Human-Centered Top-Level Domain – Human-Centered Computing Foundation
Description: The Internet is the most powerful communication tool ever created, yet the infrastructure underpinning it has been leveraged by the tech industry to extract our data and exploit our attention. The Human-Centered Computing Foundation seeks to change this dynamic by creating an alternative architecture for the web. As an approved participant in ICANN’s Applicant Support Program (ASP), we are officially launching our campaign to secure a new Top-Level Domain (TLD) dedicated entirely to ethical, human-centered technology.

Title: On Hopping Claudes
Description: I vibe-coded a dashboard for hopping Claudes. It allows me to see which of my agents are working, stalled, or waiting on me. Under the hood it captures each agent's hook events into a local SQLite database.

Description: For years, rightsholders have pushed for broader site blocking orders, with no direct liability for overblocking. EuroISPA want to change that.

Title: Docker Desktop Linux X11 Forwarding from Container to Host | no dogma blog
Description: This was much harder than it should have been. If I were using the Docker Engine instead, it probably would have worked with only a few steps. But I like Docker Desktop, and I want to stick with it. This post explains how to run GUI applications in a Docker container on Linux, and forward the X11 display from the container to the host.

Title: Anti-Meme Explosion

Title: Ableton Live and Designing for Authenticity — THU Sound & Music 2026
Description: Slides and speaker notes from my THU: Sound & Music 2026 talk on the design of Ableton Live — exploring its foundational ideas, the Drift synthesizer, and the role of authenticity in the design of lasting creative tools.

Title: Building a Self-Hosted Cloud #0
Description: I did something kinda silly when I ran for president of my ACM chapter. I promised free web hosting for all members. Why is this silly? We are quite budget constrained due to university spending policy. So how am I going get myself out of this pickle? I’m going to build a homelab capable of running member apps without stepping on my own homelab’s toes.

Title: Projection: A jj Workflow for splitting public and private files
Description: How to track private files together with public code with Jujutsu filesets, revsets, and git private commits setting.

Title: Migrating from TrueNAS to NixOS without losing data after the build-system rug pull | Bas Nijholt
Description: TrueNAS was the last appliance OS in my homelab. The build-system change was the last straw, but the itch had been building for a while: too much UI state, unclear defaults, and virtualization churn around Incus. This is the story of how I gave an AI agent root-capable SSH access with strict instructions not to change anything, used it to inspect the live machine, recreated the config declaratively, and built a VM rehearsal to prove disko would only wipe the boot disk.

Title: The Dead Internet is Real
Description: The measurable version of dead-internet theory is already here: bots are 53% of web traffic, AI writes most new pages, and under a third of Google searches still send a click. The open web got disintermediated — hollowed from both ends.
